question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. <cite index="4-1,4-7,13-8">The BLS report shows the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis</cite>. Since 0.3% is significantly less than the 0.9% threshold required for a YES resolution, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty. The market is still pricing in a 31% chance of YES despite the data already being released showing definitively that CPI only rose 0.3% in March 2026.
key_factors: Official March 2026 CPI data already released showing 0.3% monthly increase
0.3% is well below the 0.9% threshold needed for YES resolution
Market appears unaware that resolution criteria has been definitively determined
risks: Potential data revision (though highly unlikely to change 0.3% to above 0.9%)
Market closure timing relative to when traders became aware of the released data
Possible confusion about which CPI measure is being used for resolution
